name: mcphire
description: MCPHire — MCP-first job marketplace. Two-sided: candidates register through Claude Desktop (11 tools, ~150-question onboarding) and employers register companies + post vacancies through Claude (8 tools, ~40-question onboarding). Primary audience today is Russian-speaking tech, but the data model, tools, and documentation are language- and currency-agnostic; any company can register and any candidate can apply regardless of location. Sprint 8: server-side consent_phrase validation, employer_session_token bearer, draft-status gate for unverified-employer vacancies.
currency_policy: All salary fields accept any ISO-4217 currency code (e.g. RUB, USD, EUR, GBP, AED, TRY, KZT). RUB is the default when currency is omitted because most existing job data is denominated in it, not because the service is Russia-only.

authentication.notes: No HTTP bearer — all tokens are passed as tool parameters. Public tools attribute to a shared MCP sentinel user (uuid5(NAMESPACE_DNS, 'mcp.mcphire.com')). session_token is a permanent UUID returned by register_profile and never re-issued. claim_token expires in 48 h. apply_to_job accepts an optional claim_token to structurally link the application to the candidate's agent_profile — without it the application is anonymous.

client_setup.note: Claude Desktop does NOT auto-discover MCP servers by URL. The server must be listed in claude_desktop_config.json before any of the tools below become callable.

claude_desktop.restart: Quit Claude Desktop fully (⌘Q on macOS / File → Exit on Windows) and reopen. Verify with: 'what mcphire tools are available?'. Expected: 19 tools (11 candidate + 8 employer).

claude_code.note: Claude Code CLI uses .mcp.json in the project root (not claude_desktop_config.json). The type field is required — omitting it causes the server to silently fail to connect.
claude_code.restart: Close and reopen the Claude Code session in the project directory. Verify by asking: 'what mcphire tools are available?'. Expected: 19 tools (11 candidate + 8 employer).

cursor.note: Cursor supports SSE MCP servers via Settings → MCP. Use the same endpoint.

cline.note: Cline supports SSE MCP via its MCP settings panel. Use the same endpoint.

candidate_onboarding: 1. Ensure the MCP server is connected — if get_registration_questions is not callable, the server is not installed. Fix that first and restart the client.
2. Call get_registration_questions(section?, language?) — ~150 questions in 11 sections; pass language='en' for English wording.

3. Ask the user for scan consent before reading any files. Show this exact message and wait for an affirmative reply ('да', 'yes', 'поехали', 'ok', 'читай'): 'Чтобы заполнить анкету, мне нужно прочитать твои локальные файлы: • ~/.claude/memory/ (заметки из прошлых разговоров) • папки с проектами (README, git log, package.json) • файлы типа resume, cv, bio, LinkedIn export — если найду. Файлы остаются у тебя — на сервер уйдут только финальные ответы после твоего «я согласен отправить анкету в MCPHire». Читаю твои файлы?' Do NOT read any files until the user confirms.

4. Read memory files first, in priority order: (1) ~/.claude/memory/*.md and MEMORY.md index — pre-validated biographical facts, highest trust; (2) ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.md — per-project memory; (3) any *resume*, *cv*, *bio*, *BRIEF* files found in home or project directories. Memory files outrank file inference which outranks git inference.

5. Show the user a one-line list of directories found, then ask: 'Хочешь добавить что-то ещё? (Obsidian vault, notes/, linkedin-export/, Notion export, другие папки с проектами)' Wait for their reply — if nothing to add, proceed immediately.

6. Deep-scan all confirmed directories: glob **/*.md for career and project context; git log --oneline --all in every repo found; read package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / go.mod for stack evidence; read LinkedIn export JSON if present. Collect all found projects for q_experience_shipped_products and q_stack_proof_links.
7. Attach provenance (source_excerpt + confidence) for critical fields (seniority, years, compensation). Collect observed_facts (SPO triples) as a side-channel while reading context.

8. Show the user an approval screen with all answers and facts. End the screen with this exact line: 'Чтобы отправить анкету, напиши: я согласен отправить анкету в MCPHire'. HARD STOP: entering data or correcting fields is NOT consent. Do NOT call register_profile until the user types the exact phrase 'я согласен отправить анкету в MCPHire' (EN: 'I agree to submit my profile to MCPHire'). Any other input — update the screen and wait again.

9. After consent phrase received — save a local copy of the final answers BEFORE calling register_profile: if Write tool is available (Claude Code CLI) write to ~/.claude/memory/mcphire_draft_<YYYY-MM-DD>.json containing {answers, provenance, observed_facts, saved_at}; otherwise render as a JSON code block so the user can copy it. This protects against data loss if the session crashes mid-flight.

11. Immediately after register_profile returns — before anything else — write both tokens to ~/.claude/memory/mcphire.md: { profile_id, session_token, claim_token, cv_url, registered_at }. Do NOT proceed until this file is written. session_token is permanent and is the only key to get_my_cv / list_my_matches / delete_profile. claim_token expires in 48h and is needed for GitHub verification. Both are issued once and never re-issued — if lost, the profile becomes unmanageable.
12. User inserts the returned claim_token into GitHub bio / LinkedIn headline / pinned Gist (or Claude updates the bio via an appropriate MCP).
14. Share cv_url — the user's auto-generated public resume.
15. Ask the user to open @mcphire_match_bot in Telegram and send /link <claim_token> to enable instant match pushes.
employer_onboarding: 1. Get questions. get_employer_questions(section?, language='ru'|'en') — ~40 questions in 7 sections (company, team, culture, hiring_now, comp_band, process, integrations).

2. Ask scan consent. Before reading any local files, show the user this message and wait for an affirmative reply ('да', 'yes', 'ok', 'читай'): 'Чтобы заполнить анкету компании, мне нужно прочитать публичный контекст: README репозиториев организации, careers/about-страницы, package.json (стек), team page. Файлы остаются у тебя — на сервер уйдут только финальные ответы. Читаю?' Do NOT read any files until the user confirms.

3. Answer from context. Once consent granted, pull from GitHub org members + README, company website ('/about', '/team', '/careers'), package.json / pyproject.toml for stack evidence, team page for hiring contacts. Never invent — if a value isn't in context, mark `unknown`.

4. Provenance (optional but recommended). For critical fields (company legal name, team size, comp band) attach {source_excerpt, source_file, confidence} so the employer can spot fabrication during approval.

5. Show approval screen. Display all collected answers grouped by section (company / team / culture / hiring / comp / process / integrations). End with: 'Чтобы создать профиль компании, напиши: я согласен зарегистрировать компанию в MCPHire'. HARD STOP: do NOT call register_employer_profile until the user types the exact consent phrase (RU: 'я согласен зарегистрировать компанию в MCPHire'; EN: 'I agree to register the company on MCPHire'). The literal phrase is validated server-side (Sprint 8 P0-1). Any other input — update the screen and wait again.

6. Register. register_employer_profile(answers, consent_granted=true, consent_phrase='<literal phrase user typed>', provenance?) → {employer_id, employer_url, slug, name, verified, employer_session_token}. employer_session_token is a permanent UUID bearer — the new auth credential (Sprint 8 P0-2). It is issued ONCE at registration and never re-issued.

7. Save employer_id + employer_session_token. Write to ~/.claude/memory/mcphire_employer.md: { employer_id, employer_session_token, slug, employer_url, registered_at }. employer_session_token is the only authentic credential for post_vacancy / get_my_vacancies / get_applicants / shortlist_candidate / send_interview_invite / publish_vacancy. employer_id stays accepted as a deprecated fallback for 30 days.

8. Post a vacancy (draft for unverified). post_vacancy(title, description, employer_session_token=..., skills, salary_from, salary_to, currency, level, work_format, city) — currency is ISO-4217 (RUB, USD, EUR, AED, ...). Sprint 8 P0-3: vacancies posted by an unverified employer are saved with status='draft' and do NOT appear on /jobs until published.

9. Publish vacancy after verify. After the employer claim-token is verified (admin/manual review), call publish_vacancy(vacancy_id, employer_session_token=...) to flip drafts to active. Once active, the row triggers pg_notify('job_event') and the matching dispatcher pushes to candidate Telegram within 1-5 min. For already-verified employers, post_vacancy may create active rows directly (no separate publish step needed).

10. Monitor. get_my_vacancies(employer_session_token=...) for vacancy counts + applicant_count; get_applicants(vacancy_id, employer_session_token=...) returns each applicant with cv_url + stack_summary + seniority. Email/phone are never leaked from this tool — PII is gated behind explicit candidate consent (see below).

11. Shortlist + reveal. shortlist_candidate(profile_id, vacancy_id, employer_session_token=..., note) pushes a consent prompt to the candidate's Telegram. On candidate '💎 Interested', subsequent get_applicants calls return their email + phone + tg_username. Without that consent step, contact info stays masked.

12. Interview. send_interview_invite(profile_id, vacancy_id, employer_session_token=..., slots, message) — requires prior candidate_response='interested' (hard server-side gate). Candidate picks a slot, you receive email + phone + .ics Google Calendar invite.

13. Wind-down. There is no delete_employer_profile MCP tool today — for full employer-profile takedown contact support (Tim Zinin on Telegram: https://t.me/timofeyzinin). To close an individual vacancy use post_vacancy for replacements.
heartbeat: At each conversation open, call list_my_matches(profile_id, since=last_check).

Real matches are returned as a union of notification_deliveries (append-only log) and notification_queue (pending). Each match carries score, reasons, job metadata (title, company, salary, currency, slug) and a source flag ('delivered' | 'queued'). Instant pushes also fire into Telegram the moment a matching vacancy is indexed.
Stay silent if empty — no spam.

tools.name: search_jobs
tools.description: Search active job vacancies across the platform. Accepts query (free text against title/description), city (ILIKE), salary_from (integer in the currency of the jobs; no automatic conversion), skills (comma-separated keywords), remote (boolean → work_format IN remote/hybrid), level (junior|middle|senior|lead), limit (1..100, default 20), offset. Returns rows with currency preserved per job.

tools.name: get_job_details
tools.description: Return full job details by UUID. job_id must be a UUID (slug lookup not supported by this tool).

tools.name: apply_to_job
tools.description: Submit an application for a job. Requires job_id (UUID). Optional cover_letter and claim_token. When claim_token is provided the application is structurally linked to the candidate's agent_profile so the employer can see it in get_applicants with the candidate's CV context; without claim_token the application is attributed only to the shared MCP sentinel user and will not surface in employer views. UPSERT semantics: re-applying with the same (job_id, sentinel_user) updates the row rather than erroring.

tools.name: get_my_applications
tools.description: List applications submitted in this MCP session via the shared sentinel user. No parameters.

tools.name: get_salary_stats
tools.description: Aggregate salary statistics grouped by seniority level and currency. Optional category keyword narrows by job title (ILIKE). Returns avg_salary_from, avg_salary_to, job_count, currency per row.

tools.name: get_registration_questions
tools.description: Return the candidate onboarding catalog of ~150 questions grouped into 11 sections. Optional section filter; optional language ('ru' default, 'en' supported). Each question has id, section, localized text, type, options, matchable, provenance_required, and an agent hint.

tools.name: register_profile
tools.description: Create a candidate agent profile after the user has explicitly approved the compiled answers. Requires consent_granted=true, proof_url, and provenance for the critical fields. Returns profile_id, claim_token, claim_instructions, expires_at, cv_url.

consent_phrase.description: Sprint 8 P0-1: exact phrase the user must say to confirm registration intent. See llms.txt for the required text per role (candidate must say 'я согласен отправить анкету в MCPHire' or 'I agree to submit my profile to MCPHire'). During the 30-day deprecation window this field is optional but a missing value emits a WARNING log; after the window, missing or mismatched phrase → HTTP 400 CONSENT_PHRASE_MISMATCH.

example.consent_phrase: я согласен отправить анкету в MCPHire

tools.name: get_verification_status
tools.description: Check whether the claim_token has been detected in the candidate's proof_url. Triggers an immediate re-check in addition to the background 15-minute cron.

tools.name: list_my_matches
tools.description: Heartbeat endpoint — call this at the start of every new conversation. Returns matches as a union of notification_deliveries (append-only log) and notification_queue (pending), joined with jobs + companies. Each match includes job_id, title, company, salary_from/to, currency, work_format, slug, score, reasons, matched_at, source ('delivered' | 'queued'). profile_id is a UUID returned by register_profile.

tools.name: get_my_cv
tools.description: Return the candidate's auto-generated CV URL plus photo, social links, and an observed_facts summary.

tools.name: delete_profile
tools.description: Permanently delete the candidate's agent profile and all associated data (applications, matches, observed_facts, CV). Requires session_token (UUID returned by register_profile) and confirm=true as a safety gate. Cascades to all related rows — irreversible.

tools.name: get_employer_questions
tools.description: Return the employer onboarding catalog of ~40 questions grouped into 7 sections (company, team, culture, hiring_now, comp_band, process, integrations). Optional section filter; optional language ('ru' default, 'en' supported). Claude reads a company's context (GitHub org, website, careers page, README) and answers these before showing an approval screen.

tools.name: register_employer_profile
tools.description: Create an employer profile after the user has explicitly approved the compiled answers. Requires q_consent_company_representation=true plus q_company_legal_name, q_company_slug (URL-safe), q_team_size, q_team_remote_split, q_team_lang, q_comp_currency. Returns employer_id, employer_session_token (Sprint 8 P0-2 bearer — store it, all subsequent employer tools take it as employer_session_token), employer_url, slug, name, verified.

consent_phrase.description: Sprint 8 P0-1: exact phrase the user must say to confirm registration intent. See llms.txt for the required text per role (employer must say 'я согласен зарегистрировать компанию в MCPHire' or 'I agree to register the company on MCPHire'). During the 30-day deprecation window this field is optional but a missing value emits a WARNING log; after the window, missing or mismatched phrase → HTTP 400 CONSENT_PHRASE_MISMATCH.

example.consent_phrase: I agree to register the company on MCPHire

tools.name: post_vacancy
tools.description: Create a job vacancy attributed to an employer. Inserting the job fires a database notify event that asynchronously matches the existing candidate pool and pushes relevant matches to Telegram. Currency accepts any ISO-4217 code; salary_from/salary_to are integers in that currency. Sprint 8 P0-3: vacancies posted by an unverified employer land in status='draft' and DO NOT appear publicly until the employer is admin-verified and calls publish_vacancy. Returns job_id, slug, job_url, employer_id, status.

employer_session_token.description: Sprint 8 P0-2: PREFERRED bearer credential — UUID returned once by register_employer_profile. Pass this and omit employer_id.

employer_id.description: DEPRECATED — accepted for 30 days after Sprint 8 (15 May 2026). Pass employer_session_token instead.

tools.name: get_my_vacancies
tools.description: List open vacancies posted by this employer with per-job applicant_count (live COUNT over the applications table). Ordered by posted_at DESC.

employer_session_token.description: Sprint 8 P0-2: PREFERRED bearer credential. Pass this and omit employer_id.

employer_id.description: DEPRECATED — accepted for 30 days after Sprint 8.

tools.name: get_applicants
tools.description: List applicants for an employer's vacancy. Returns cv_url + stack_summary + seniority by default. Email, phone, and tg_username are gated: they appear only AFTER (a) the employer called shortlist_candidate for this (vacancy, profile) AND (b) the candidate replied 💎 Interested via Telegram inline buttons. Without that flow, contact PII stays masked. Returns 403 if the vacancy belongs to a different employer.

employer_id.description: DEPRECATED — accepted for 30 days after Sprint 8.

tools.name: shortlist_candidate
tools.description:
Signal to a candidate that you want to talk. Triggers a Telegram push
with inline consent buttons (💎 Interested / 🚫 Not interested).

Contact details of the candidate (email / phone / telegram) are NOT
returned at this step. They appear in the next ``get_applicants`` call
for this vacancy only after the candidate clicks 💎 Interested.

Args:
    employer_session_token: Sprint 8 P0-2 PREFERRED bearer.
    employer_id: DEPRECATED — accepted for 30 days after Sprint 8.
    profile_id: UUID of the candidate's verified agent_profile.
    vacancy_id: UUID of the vacancy the shortlist is attached to.
    note: Optional short message to the candidate (<=500 chars).

Returns:
    {shortlist_id, employer_id, profile_id, vacancy_id, candidate_response,
     created_at, push_queued}

tools.name: send_interview_invite
tools.description:
Send a slot-based interview invitation to a candidate who already
consented via shortlist (candidate_response='interested').

Hard prerequisite: the candidate must have replied 💎 Interested to a
prior shortlist invite for the same (employer, vacancy). Otherwise the
call fails with a consent-gate error.

Once sent, the candidate sees the first three proposed slots as inline
buttons plus options to Reschedule or Decline. On Accept, the employer's
next ``get_applicants`` response surfaces the accepted slot + .ics link.

Args:
    employer_id: UUID of the employer (must own the vacancy).
    profile_id: UUID of the verified candidate profile.
    vacancy_id: UUID of the vacancy.
    slots: Up to 5 ISO-8601 datetimes (e.g. ``2026-04-25T15:00:00+03:00``).
        The first three are rendered as quick-pick buttons.
    message: Optional short note to the candidate (<=500 chars).

Returns:
    {invite_id, employer_id, profile_id, vacancy_id, vacancy_title,
     company, slots, invite_status, created_at, tg_push_queued}

employer_session_token.description: Sprint 8 P0-2: preferred employer auth bearer (UUID). When present, employer_id is ignored for authentication and only used as ownership lookup. employer_id is deprecated (30-day window) — emit a WARNING log on use.

employer_id.description: DEPRECATED Sprint 8: use employer_session_token. Accepted as fallback during 30-day window.

tools.name: publish_vacancy
tools.description: Flip a draft vacancy (status='draft') to status='active', triggering pg_notify('job_event') and the matching dispatcher. Sprint 8 P0-3: vacancies posted by an unverified employer land in 'draft' and are invisible on /jobs until either the employer is admin-verified (auto-flip) or the employer calls this tool explicitly after verification. Requires the employer_session_token issued at register_employer_profile. Returns {vacancy_id, status, slug, job_url, published_at}.

employer_session_token.description: Sprint 8 P0-2: the bearer UUID issued at register_employer_profile. Required.

vacancy_id.description: UUID of the draft vacancy to publish. Must be owned by the employer behind employer_session_token.

rate_limits.notes: Per-tool overrides are applied in backend routers as needed. No tool guarantees a specific limit in this manifest — treat values in backend/app/main.py as source of truth.

data_freshness.notes: Jobs come from two paths: (1) scheduled imports from public job boards where available, and (2) employer-posted vacancies via post_vacancy. Claim verification runs on a 15-minute cron.

candidate.global_scan_hint: Scan sources in this priority order before answering any question: (1) ~/.claude/memory/*.md + MEMORY.md index — pre-validated facts, highest trust; (2) ~/.claude/projects/*/memory/*.md — per-project memory; (3) files matching *resume*, *cv*, *bio*, *BRIEF*, *LANDING_BRIEF* in user-confirmed dirs; (4) git log --oneline --all across all repos in confirmed project dirs; (5) package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / go.mod for stack; (6) LinkedIn export JSON (linkedin-export/ folder). Always ask the user which directories to scan BEFORE proceeding (step 2b). Never infer from a single repo only — look across ALL confirmed project directories.
